cancer_history_summary:
Age: 71
Sex: Female
Cancer type: Glioblastoma
Histology: IDH-wildtype, WHO Grade 4
Current extent: Progressive (diffuse infiltrative disease in left temporal and right parietal lobes)
Biomarkers: IDH-wildtype, GFAP positive, Olig2 positive, Ki-67 30-38%, ATRX retained, p53 wild-type, EGFR focal gene amplification, PDGFRA amplification (copy number approx 8), CDK4 amplification (copy number approx 6), MTAP homozygous deletion, NF1 nonsense substitution (p.Arg251*), TERT promoter mutation (c.-124C>T), MGMT promoter unmethylated, TMB 4 mutations per megabase, MSI negative
Treatment history:
# 2020: Maximal surgical debulking of left temporal lobe
# 2020: Concurrent temozolomide and external beam intensity-modulated radiotherapy (60 Gy/30 fractions)
# 2020-2021: Adjuvant temozolomide
# 2021-2022: Bevacizumab; treatment interrupted for grade 3 proteinuria
# 2022: Re-resection of left temporal lobe
# 2025-02-12: Stereotactic-guided craniotomy with gross-total resection of recurrent left temporal glioblastoma
# 2025-2025-08: PDGFR alpha tyrosine kinase inhibitor and metronomic temozolomide; best response stable disease
# 2025-08-2026-05-03: Bevacizumab; discontinued due to disease progression and functional decline
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: ECOG 4. Essential hypertension (grade 3 during bevacizumab), hyperlipidemia, hypothyroidism, osteoarthritis of knees, history of bevacizumab-induced grade 3 proteinuria, expressive aphasia, dysphagia, and encephalopathy.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 52
Sex: Female
Cancer type: Breast cancer
Histology: Infiltrating ductal carcinoma
Current extent: Metastatic (liver, lungs, and L4 vertebra). Most recent tumor marker CA 27-29 38 U/mL (2026-05-14), trending favorably.
Biomarkers: ER 80%, PR 70%, HER2 IHC 0, Ki-67 22%, PIK3CA p.H1047R, ESR1 p.D538G, TP53 p.R175H, GATA3 p.R361C, CDK4 amplification, FGFR1 amplification, BRCA1 wild-type, BRCA2 wild-type, AKT1 wild-type, KRAS wild-type, NRAS wild-type, BRAF wild-type, MSI stable, TMB 4 mut/Mb
Treatment history:
# 2021-06-23-2023-09-09: letrozole and ribociclib; best response partial response (confirmed 2022-07-28)
# 2023-09-09-2024: letrozole; ribociclib discontinued due to hepatotoxicity
# 2024-2025: fulvestrant and everolimus; achieved partial response (hepatic lesions decreased, pulmonary nodules stable)
# 2025-2025-09-28: fulvestrant and zoledronic acid; developed progression in L4 vertebra and liver
# 2025-09-28-present: alpelisib and fulvestrant; achieved partial response (liver lesion decreased to 0.6 cm, improved L4 lesion)
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: Hypertension, hyperlipidemia, Grade 2 ribociclib-associated hepatotoxicity (2023-09-09), Grade 2 everolimus-associated stomatitis and hyperglycemia, Grade 1 zoledronic acid infusion reaction, alpelisib-associated maculopapular rash (resolved), Grade 1-2 transaminitis, ECOG 1.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 68
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Lung cancer
Histology: Small cell carcinoma
Current extent: Metastatic (extensive-stage). Involvement of right lower lobe, mediastinal lymph nodes, L3 vertebra, right iliac crest, left adrenal gland, right femur, and liver (newly identified 2025-10-07). Most recent LDH 480 U/L (increasing trend as of 2025-10-07).
Biomarkers: Synaptophysin positive, CD56 positive, Chromogranin A focal weak positive, Cytokeratin AE1/AE3 positive, Ki-67 85%, TTF-1 negative, PD-L1 <=1%, TP53 p.Val157Phe, RB1 exons 9-20 homozygous deletion, MYC amplification, STK11 p.Phe354Leu, CREBBP p.Arg1621His. Negative for EGFR, KRAS, NRAS, HRAS, ALK, ROS1, RET, NTRK1/2/3.
Treatment history:
# December 2022-October 2023: carboplatin and etoposide; best response partial response
# January 2023: Palliative radiation (single 8 Gy fraction) to L3 vertebra
# December 2023-January 2024: Prophylactic cranial irradiation (20 Gy in 5 fractions)
# November 2024-June 2025: amrubicin; best response stable disease
# June 2025-October 2025: lurbinectedin; best response partial response

Age: 21
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Osteosarcoma
Histology: Conventional high-grade osteosarcoma
Current extent: Metastatic (solitary right lower lobe pulmonary nodule, most recent size 1.6 cm as of 2024-11-14, increasing from 1.4 cm)
Biomarkers: SATB2 positive, Vimentin positive, Osteocalcin positive, Cytokeratin AE1/AE3 negative, S-100 negative, EMA negative, p63/p40 negative, TP53 p.Arg175His, PTEN p.Arg233*, RB1 homozygous loss, MDM2 amplification (6-fold), CDK4 amplification (5-fold), ERBB2 (HER2) amplification (8-fold), TMB 3.2 mut/Mb, MSI-stable, KRAS p.Gly12Asp (VUS), NF1 frameshift (VUS), ATRX frameshift (VUS)
Treatment history:
# 2020-10 to 2021-01: methotrexate, doxorubicin, cisplatin; response greater than 90 percent necrosis on imaging
# 2021-06-01: Wide resection of right distal femur with endoprosthetic reconstruction; negative margins
# 2021-06 to 2021-08: methotrexate, doxorubicin, cisplatin; achieved no evidence of disease
# 2022-11 to 2023-04: ifosfamide, etoposide; progressed
# 2023-09 to present: pembrolizumab; partial response (nodule decreased to 1.4 cm as of 2023-11-15), slow progression to 1.6 cm as of 2024-11-14
# 2024: Stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) 30 Gy in 5 fractions to right lung lesion
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: ECOG 0-1. History of Grade 2 immune-mediated colitis (resolved). Right distal femur endoprosthesis. Chronic mild leukopenia.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 64
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Gastro-esophageal junction cancer
Histology: Poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma, signet-ring cell type
Current extent: Metastatic (liver, peritoneum, retroperitoneal lymph nodes, L2 vertebra). Most recent CEA 18 ng/mL (rising). Malignant ascites present.
Biomarkers: HER2 IHC 3+, HER2 ISH amplified (copy number 9), Microsatellite Stable (MLH1, PMS2, MSH2, MSH6 retained), TMB 5 muts/Mb, TP53 p.Arg175His, CDH1 p.Glu738*, KRAS p.Gly12Asp, PIK3CA p.His1047Arg, ARID1A loss of heterozygosity, MET amplification (copy number 6), FGFR2 amplification (copy number 4), SMAD4 p.Arg361Cys, PD-L1 CPS 15
Treatment history:
# 2020-09-19 to 2021-05-01: capecitabine + oxaliplatin + trastuzumab; best response partial metabolic response of primary tumor and stable disease in liver
# 2021-05-01 to 2021-10-13: 5-fluorouracil + leucovorin + irinotecan; best response progressive disease (peritoneal carcinomatosis)
# 2023-06-07: Palliative laparoscopic gastro-jejunostomy
# 2024-01 to 2024-02: Palliative external-beam radiotherapy to L2 vertebra; best response partial response
# 2024-07 to 2025-02: pembrolizumab; best response stable disease, subsequently progressed
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: ECOG 1. Hypertension. Hyperlipidemia. Former smoker (10 pack-years). Immune-related hypothyroidism (Grade 2). Grade 3 anemia (required transfusion). Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy. HBV immune. HIV negative. HCV negative.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 70
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Prostate cancer
Histology: Adenocarcinoma
Current extent: Metastatic (T10 vertebra, left ischial wing, right adrenal gland, left scapula, left femur). Most recent PSA 3.0 ng/mL (increasing trend from nadir of 1.5 ng/mL).
Biomarkers: PSA positive, NKX3.1 positive, AMACR positive, ERG FISH positive (TMPRSS2-ERG fusion), Ki-67 45%, AR amplification, PTEN homozygous deletion, TP53 p.Arg248Trp, BRCA2 p.Ser2252*, SPOP p.Phe133Ser, CDK12 p.Arg1049*, FOXA1 p.Asn335Ser, ATM p.Asp2913Asn, CHEK2 p.Thr367Profs*5, RB1 loss, MSI stable, TMB 4.2 muts/Mb.
Treatment history:
# June 2020: Robotic-assisted radical prostatectomy with extended pelvic lymphadenectomy
# October 2020-present: leuprolide acetate
# March 2021-April 2021: abiraterone acetate + prednisone; best response partial response (PSA 0.4 ng/mL)
# April 2021-November 2022: enzalutamide
# November 2022-October 2023: apalutamide
# October 2023-present: zoledronic acid
# December 2023-present: rucaparib + enzalutamide (clinical trial); best response partial response (PSA 1.5 ng/mL)
# April 2026-present: denosumab
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: ECOG 1. Hypertension (controlled on amlodipine). Hyperlipidemia (managed with pravastatin). Former smoker (quit >20 years ago). History of Grade 2 seizure-like event (attributed to enzalutamide). History of Grade 3 thrombocytopenia (resolved). History of Grade 2 nausea (attributed to rucaparib).

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 76
Sex: Female
Cancer type: Ovarian cancer
Histology: High-grade serous carcinoma
Current extent: Metastatic (FIGO Stage IVB). Most recent CA-125 48 U/mL (stable). As of 2026-05-10, imaging shows new subcentimeter peritoneal nodules (<= 5 mm) in the paracolic gutters, pouch of Douglas, and omentum, indicating early disease progression. Hepatic disease is stable with a dominant right lobe lesion measuring 3.5 cm with central necrosis following ablation.
Biomarkers: PAX8 positive, WT-1 positive, CK7 strong diffuse positive, p53 aberrant overexpression (null pattern), Ki-67 70 percent, estrogen receptor focal weak positive, progesterone receptor negative, HER2 negative (Score 0-1+), BRCA2 pathogenic frameshift (p.Ser1982Argfs*22), TP53 missense (p.Arg248Gln), ATM homozygous loss, MYC amplification, HRD score 78 (positive), TMB 4.2 mut/Mb (low), MSI stable.
Treatment history:
# 2021-02-08: Optimal cytoreductive laparotomy (total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, infracolic omentectomy)
# 2022-07-2022: carboplatin and paclitaxel (6 cycles); best response partial response
# 2022-08-2023-08-18: niraparib; best response stable disease
# 2025-02-2025-08: trabectedin; best response progressive hepatic disease
# 2025-10-present: rucaparib; best response stable disease
# 2026-01: Percutaneous radiofrequency ablation of dominant hepatic metastasis and stereotactic body radiation therapy to isolated para-aortic lymph node
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: ECOG 1. History of Grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy (persistent, limited to toes). History of Grade 3 thrombocytopenia (resolved). History of Grade 2 anemia (chronic, most recent hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL). History of Grade 2 transaminitis (occurred during trabectedin therapy, resolved).

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 70
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Cutaneous melanoma
Histology: Superficial spreading melanoma with focal epithelioid cells
Current extent: Metastatic. Evidence of pulmonary metastases (stable partial response) and progressive hepatic metastases (largest 3.7 cm as of 2026-04-10, increased by 45% by 2026-05-08). Most recent LDH 310 U/L (2026-05-08), showing an overall increasing trend.
Biomarkers: BRAF V600E, TERT promoter -124C>T, CDKN2A homozygous deletion, TP53 R175H, NF1 Q1589*, PD-L1 TPS 15%, Ki-67 35%, TMB 14 mut/Mb, MSI-stable, KRAS G12R (subclonal), PIK3CA H1047R (subclonal), STK11 p.Phe97Leufs*13 (subclonal), NRAS wild-type
Treatment history:
# 2021-02-24: excisional biopsy of left upper extremity
# 2023-06-14: wide local excision of left upper extremity and sentinel lymph node biopsy of left axilla
# 2023-09-15-2024-11-27: pembrolizumab; completed 12 months
# 2024-11-27-2025-10-05: ipilimumab and nivolumab; achieved partial response in pulmonary metastases; ipilimumab discontinued due to grade 3 colitis
# 2025-10-05-2026-05-08: nivolumab; maintained partial response in lungs, but developed progressive hepatic metastases
